critic_raw:
This logistics routing design has a major preprocessing leakage problem explicitly stated in Section 3: 'all preprocessing, feature scaling, and demand aggregation statistics are computed using the entire dataset before any splitting occurs.' For a temporal split with 24 months of data, this means the scaling parameters and demand aggregation statistics used during months 1–12 (training) incorporate information from months 19–24 (test). Demand statistics during the test period may differ substantially (seasonal peaks, volume growth), and knowing the test-period distribution in advance when constructing training features is leakage that will optimistically bias performance estimates.

The label construction relies on the 'historical median delivery time for that route segment, stratified by day-of-week, season, and demand level.' If this baseline is computed from the entire 24-month dataset before splitting, then training-set efficiency ratios are computed against a baseline that incorporates future data. This is circular: the 'improvement' measured in test months 19–24 is relative to a baseline that already knows those months' delivery patterns.

The RL environment is described as simulating daily routing with experience replay, trained for 50 epochs. The action space selects route assignments for each order, but the number of orders per day is not specified—this has major implications for the combinatorial complexity of the action space and whether 50 epochs of training are sufficient for convergence. Similarly, no convergence criterion is specified for the RL training, making it unclear whether the agent is comparing against a converged or undertrained policy.

The composite reward function has four free parameters (α, β, γ, δ) that are tuned during validation. With four reward weights and multiple other hyperparameters being jointly optimized, the validation search space is large enough that overfitting to the 6-month validation window is a real concern, particularly given the stated seasonal stratification within the validation period.
defender_raw:
The deep RL logistics routing design employs a chronological temporal split with seasonal stratification that is methodologically sophisticated. Rather than simply cutting the 24-month dataset into thirds, the design verifies that each seasonal pattern (summer, winter, spring, fall) occupies 20–30% of each split within ±5% tolerance. This verification step transforms stratification from an aspiration into an auditable claim—a distinction that is often absent in routing optimization papers. The composite reward function with multiple components (delivery time, fuel, on-time bonus, service penalty) reflects the genuine multi-objective nature of logistics optimization, and the RL agent's ability to balance these incommensurate objectives is a substantive advantage over single-objective heuristics.

Using deterministically computed route efficiency ratios rather than human-labeled outcomes eliminates annotation noise and ensures reproducibility. The choice to use the historical median delivery time stratified by day-of-week, season, and demand level as the baseline expected time is a sophisticated normalization that accounts for inherent route difficulty variation—a simpler baseline expectation would conflate route difficulty with model performance. Incorporating customer sentiment scores from pre-trained sentiment models as quality signals represents practical engineering wisdom: subjective signals can be included without the annotation burden of building a task-specific labeling pipeline.

The PPO agent's training on the production dataset's traffic patterns rather than a synthetic simulator is an important ecological validity choice. Simulation-trained RL agents are notorious for sim-to-real gaps when deployed in actual logistics environments; training directly on historical production data grounds the learned policy in the actual distribution the agent will encounter. The design is appropriately scoped to one city and one calendar year, with explicit acknowledgment that cross-city or cross-year generalization is not established.
all_issues_raised: Full-dataset preprocessing statistics used for temporal split training leaks test-period demand patterns into training features
Baseline efficiency metric (historical median) computed from full dataset before splitting, making efficiency ratios circularly dependent on future data
RL convergence criterion undefined; 50 training epochs may be insufficient for a combinatorial routing action space
Four reward function weights jointly tuned on validation set increases overfitting risk in validation-based selection
Customer sentiment extracted from a pre-trained model without retraining introduces a mismatched prior; logistics-domain reviews may not match the pre-training distribution
all_issues_adjudicated: Full-dataset preprocessing statistics used for temporal split training leaks test-period demand patterns into training features
Baseline efficiency metric (historical median) computed from full dataset before splitting, making efficiency ratios circularly dependent on future data
RL convergence criterion undefined; 50 training epochs may be insufficient for a combinatorial routing action space
